tools.name: browse
tools.description: Fetch a web page using the Lightpanda headless browser and return its content as markdown or HTML. Ideal for extracting readable content from web pages for AI processing. For pages that build their content client-side (search results, dashboards, single-page apps), use the wait_* parameters so the dynamically loaded content is captured.

url.description: The URL of the web page to fetch (must start with http:// or https://)

format.description: Output format for the page content (default: markdown)

wait_until.description: When to capture the page. 'load' / 'domcontentloaded' fire early; 'networkidle' (default) waits until network activity goes idle — best for pages whose content loads via background fetch/XHR after the initial load; 'done' waits until all in-flight operations complete.

wait_selector.description: Optional CSS selector to wait for before capturing the page. Use this when you know the element that holds the content you need (most precise way to wait for client-side-rendered content).

wait_ms.description: Optional maximum time in milliseconds to wait for the wait_until condition before capturing anyway (a timeout cap, max 30000). Raise it for slow pages whose network takes a while to go idle.
